fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Iowa Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Iowa - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity iowa, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to iowa, speech-language pathologist compact iowa, iowa speech-language pathologist endorsement application

board.name: Board of Speech Pathology and Audiology
board.parent_dept: Iowa Department of Inspections, Appeals and Licensing
board.mailing_address:
Iowa Department of Health and Human Services
Professional Licensure
321 East 12th Street
Des Moines, IA 50319-0075

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: The full credential to independently practice speech-language pathology in Iowa.

license_types.title: Provisional SLP License
license_types.description: Issued to SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.

steps.title: Create an Account and Apply Online
steps.description: Go to the MyIowa portal (myiowa.gov), create an account if you don't have one, and complete the online application for a Speech-Language Pathologist license, including paying the initial application fee.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for your official graduate transcripts, showing a master's or doctorate in Speech-Language Pathology from an accredited college or university, to be sent directly to the Iowa Department of Health and Human Services.

steps.title: Provide Verification of Clinical Experience or ASHA CCC
steps.description: Submit either a completed Verification of Supervised Clinical Experience Form (SLP-2) documenting 1225 clock hours of supervised experience, or official documentation of your Certificate of Clinical Competence (CCC) from the American Speech-Language-Hearing Association (ASHA).

steps.title: Submit Praxis Examination Scores
steps.description: Request that your official scores from the Praxis examination in Speech-Language Pathology (e.g., Praxis 5181) be sent directly to the Iowa Department of Health and Human Services.

steps.title: Await License Issuance
steps.description: Once all required documents and fees are received and processed by the Iowa Department of Health and Human Services, your license will be issued. You can check the status of your application through the MyIowa portal.

documents.detail: Score of 162 or higher on test 5331.

telepractice_text: Iowa permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.
provisional_text: Iowa issues a Provisional SLP License for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.
reciprocity_text: Iowa is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact. SLPs from other compact member states can practice in Iowa through a compact privilege.

faqs.question: How much does an Iowa SLP license cost?
faqs.answer: The total initial cost is approximately $120. Biennial renewal is $120.

faqs.question: Is Iowa part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
faqs.answer: Yes. Iowa has enacted ASLP-IC legislation.

faqs.question: Does Iowa allow telepractice for SLPs?
faqs.answer: Yes. Iowa permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and compact privilege holders.

faqs.question: Does Iowa have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Iowa participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Iowa without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Iowa?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Iowa while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Iowa is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact. SLPs from other compact member states can practice in Iowa through a compact privilege.

hero.title: Become a Speech-Language Pathologist in Iowa
hero.dek: To apply for a Speech-Language Pathologist license in Iowa, use the official MyIowa online portal provided by the Iowa Department of Health and Human Services.

variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Visit the MyIowa portal (myiowa.gov), click 'Login,' then 'Create Account' to register with your email, create a password, and provide necessary personal information. Once logged in, navigate to 'Professional Licensure' to find and start your application for a Speech-Language Pathologist license.
